cards.title: Migraine — Evidence-Based Naturopathic Prevention
cards._legacyHtml:
SupplementDoseReduction in frequencyEvidenceNotes
Magnesium (glycinate/citrate)400–600mg/day41% reduction★★★★ Strong (4 RCTs)Especially effective in menstrual migraine and aura
Riboflavin (B2)400mg/day50% reduction in 59% of patients★★★★ StrongTakes 3 months for full effect — urine turns bright yellow (harmless)
CoQ10300mg/day48% reduction★★★ GoodMitochondrial energy — especially in children and adolescents
Feverfew (Tanacetum)50–150mg dried leaf/day24% reduction★★★ GoodInhibits platelet aggregation and serotonin release; do not stop abruptly
Melatonin3mg at bedtimeComparable to amitriptyline★★★ GoodEspecially useful when migraine disrupts sleep
Omega-33g EPA+DHA/day30–40% frequency reduction★★★ GoodAnti-inflammatory, reduces prostaglandin cascade
T
Trigger Identification (HALT) Hunger (fasting >5hrs), Anger/stress, Lack of sleep, Tiredness/fatigue. Additional: alcohol (red wine/beer), aged cheese, MSG, nitrates, hormonal changes, bright light, strong smells, barometric pressure changes. Migraine diary for 8 weeks identifies personal triggers.
A
Acute Management Dark, quiet room. Peppermint oil 10% to temples (= paracetamol 1g for tension headache). Ice pack to neck. Ginger tea (anti-emetic, anti-inflammatory — 500mg ginger = sumatriptan in one trial). Hydration. If aura: aspirin 900mg at onset.

cards.title: Peripheral Neuropathy — Naturopathic Protocol
cards._legacyHtml:
InterventionMechanismDoseEvidence
Alpha lipoic acid ★Antioxidant, improves nerve conduction velocity600–1200mg/day (IV 600mg in acute diabetic neuropathy)★★★★ Strong — ALADIN trial
B vitamins (B1, B6, B12)Nerve myelin maintenance, conductionB1 100mg + B6 100mg + B12 (methylcobalamin) 1000mcg/day★★★ Good — benfotiamine (fat-soluble B1) preferred
Acetyl-L-carnitineNerve regeneration, mitochondrial energy1000mg TDS★★★ Good — especially chemotherapy-induced
MagnesiumNerve conduction, NMDA receptor modulation400mg/day Mg glycinate★★★ Good — especially for pain
Topical capsaicin 0.075%Depletes substance P from nerve endingsApply QID for 4–6 weeks★★★ Good — burning on application (warn patient)
Evening primrose oilGLA → improves nerve conduction in DM neuropathy4–6g/day★★★ Good for diabetic neuropathy
🟡 Diabetic Neuropathy Priority
For diabetic peripheral neuropathy — the most common type in BNYS practice: (1) Glycaemic control is PARAMOUNT — every 1% ↓ HbA1c reduces neuropathy progression 60%. (2) Alpha lipoic acid 600mg/day has Grade A evidence — start early. (3) Methylcobalamin preferred over cyanocobalamin — better CNS penetration. (4) Check B12 in all diabetics on metformin — metformin blocks B12 absorption and worsens neuropathy. (5) Foot inspection daily — neuropathy + foot ulcer = serious.

cards.title: Anxiety & Insomnia — Naturopathic Approach
cards._legacyHtml:
Herb/SupplementAnxietyInsomniaDoseEvidence
Ashwagandha (KSM-66)✅ Strong✅ Good300mg BD★★★★ Reduces cortisol, GAD-7 scores, improves sleep quality
Lavender (Silexan)✅ Strong✅ Good80mg/day standardised oral★★★★ Silexan = comparable to lorazepam in GAD (RCT)
Magnesium glycinate✅ Good✅ Good300–400mg at night★★★ Especially in deficiency — cofactor for GABA
L-theanine✅ Good✅ Moderate200–400mg★★★ Promotes alpha brainwave state — focus without sedation
Valerian root✅ Moderate✅ Good300–600mg 1 hr before bed★★★ Reduces sleep latency — takes 2–4 weeks for full effect
Passionflower✅ Good✅ Good250–500mg or tea★★★ GABA-A modulation, comparable to oxazepam (small RCT)
GABA (oral)✅ Moderate✅ Moderate100–200mg★★ Controversy re: BBB penetration, but functional effects seen

cards.title: Memory & Cognitive Health — Naturopathic Prevention
cards._legacyHtml:
Dementia is 40% Preventable
Lancet Commission 2020: 12 modifiable risk factors account for 40% of dementia cases worldwide. Naturopathic medicine addresses most of these.
InterventionDoseMechanismEvidence
Aerobic exercise150 min/week + 2x resistanceBDNF up, hippocampal neurogenesis, cerebrovascular upStrongest single intervention
MIND dietLifelong patternAntioxidants, anti-inflammatory, gut-brain axis53% reduced Alzheimer risk
Omega-3 DHA2g DHA/day (algae preferred)Neuronal membrane fluidity, anti-neuroinflammationGood for prevention
Lion's Mane mushroom500–1000mg extract TDSNGF (nerve growth factor) synthesis stimulationGood — mild cognitive impairment RCT
Bacopa monnieri300–450mg standardised/daySynaptic transmission up, acetylcholinesterase inhibitionGood — improves memory acquisition
Sleep 7–8 hoursConsistent scheduleGlymphatic clearance of amyloid-b and tau during deep sleepCritical — poor sleep raises Alzheimer risk 25%
Social engagementDaily meaningful interactionCognitive reserve, neuroplasticityIsolation raises dementia risk 60%
🟡 The Glymphatic System
Discovered in 2012, the glymphatic system clears brain waste including amyloid-b and tau proteins during deep sleep (NREM3) — 10x more active than waking. CSF flows through periarterial spaces, exchanges with interstitial fluid. Chronic sleep deprivation leads to amyloid accumulation and Alzheimer risk. Sleep on your SIDE (lateral position increases glymphatic flow 25% vs supine). This is why sleep is the most important dementia prevention intervention available.

cards.title: Homocysteine, Neuropathy & Naturopathic Neurology
cards._legacyHtml:
ConditionNaturopathic PriorityKey InterventionsEvidence
Elevated homocysteineStroke, MI, dementia risk factor — easily treatedMethylcobalamin B12 500mcg + Methylfolate 800mcg + B6 100mg daily — reduces homocysteine 25–30%Strong — reduces stroke risk 10–20%
Diabetic neuropathyMost common neuropathy in BNYS practiceAlpha lipoic acid 600mg/day (Grade A) + Methylcobalamin + Evening primrose oil 4g + Glycaemic controlStrong for ALA (ALADIN trial)
Restless Legs SyndromeIron deficiency most common causeCheck ferritin — target >75 µg/L. Iron bisglycinate if deficient. Mg glycinate 400mg at night. Folate. Dopamine support.Good for iron/Mg
Multiple sclerosis supportAdjunct — never replace DMTsVit D3 10,000 IU (under supervision), omega-3 3g, biotin 100mg (progressive MS), lipoic acid, cannabis-based medicines (physician Rx)Good for Vit D and omega-3
Bell's palsyAcute — early treatment criticalRefer for prednisolone (within 72hrs), Vit B12 IM, acupuncture for recovery, eye protectionGood for B12 + acupuncture adjunct
🟡 B12 in Neurological Disease
Methylcobalamin (NOT cyanocobalamin) is the preferred form for neurological conditions — better CNS penetration and direct cofactor without conversion. B12 deficiency causes: subacute combined degeneration of spinal cord (posterior + lateral column), peripheral neuropathy, cognitive decline, depression. High-risk groups: vegans, elderly (achlorhydria), metformin users, PPI users, pernicious anaemia. Test: serum B12 (below 300 pmol/L = treat even if technically normal). Also check methylmalonic acid and homocysteine for functional deficiency at borderline levels.

cards.title: Parkinson's Disease — Naturopathic Adjunct Care
cards._legacyHtml:
Parkinson's Disease — Medical Management Essential ★
PD is a progressive neurodegenerative disorder requiring neurologist supervision. Levodopa/carbidopa and other dopaminergic agents are essential — naturopathic interventions are adjuncts to slow progression, manage symptoms, and improve quality of life.
FeatureDetails
Cardinal motor features (TRAP)Tremor (resting, 4–6Hz, pill-rolling), Rigidity (cogwheel), Akinesia/Bradykinesia, Postural instability (late)
Non-motor features ★Constipation (begins 10–20 yrs before motor symptoms), anosmia, REM sleep behaviour disorder, depression, cognitive decline, autonomic dysfunction
DiagnosisClinical — UK Brain Bank criteria. DaTscan (dopamine transporter SPECT) for confirmation. No blood test or biomarker.
InterventionEvidenceProtocol
Exercise ★★★★Strongest neuroprotective intervention. BDNF ↑, neuroplasticity, motor function preservation150 min/week aerobic + twice-weekly resistance. Tai chi (balance +++), boxing (HIIT for PD — Rock Steady Boxing), dance therapy. Start early — more effective in early-stage PD
CoQ10★★★ Good1200mg/day — mitochondrial support. PD = mitochondrial dysfunction in substantia nigra. Early trials showed slowed progression (not replicated in large RCT)
Green tea (EGCG)★★★ Good — observational + lab3–5 cups/day or EGCG 300–400mg/day. Neuroprotective — MAO-B inhibition, iron chelation, antioxidant
Mucuna pruriens ★★★★ GoodNatural levodopa source — 15% L-DOPA content. 15–30g seed powder. Discuss with neurologist — interacts with levodopa medications. Faster onset, possibly smoother than synthetic levodopa.
Mediterranean diet★★★ GoodReduces PD risk 25–30% in observational studies. Anti-neuroinflammatory, antioxidant
Constipation management★★★★ CriticalPrebiotics/probiotics — gut-brain axis. Psyllium + magnesium + hydration. Constipation = major non-motor burden and affects levodopa absorption
🟡 Protein & Levodopa Interaction
High-protein meals significantly reduce levodopa absorption — amino acids compete for the same intestinal transporter (LAT1). Practical advice for patients on levodopa: take medication 30–60 min BEFORE meals. Distribute protein intake to evening meal (when motor control is less critical). Avoid large protein-rich meals around "on" periods. This simple dietary modification can significantly improve motor fluctuations. Also: Vit B6 at doses >50mg can reduce levodopa efficacy — use P-5-P (pyridoxal-5-phosphate) form if supplementing.

cards.title: Epilepsy & Seizure Management — Naturopathic Reference
cards._legacyHtml:
CRITICAL — Never Reduce Anti-Epileptic Drugs (AEDs) Without Neurologist
Abrupt AED withdrawal risks status epilepticus — life-threatening. Naturopathic interventions are adjuncts. Always disclose ALL supplements to the patient's neurologist — many herbs lower seizure threshold or interact with AEDs.
Seizure ClassificationFeaturesCommon AEDs
Focal (partial) — awareLocalised — sensory, motor, autonomic, psychic. Consciousness preservedCarbamazepine, lamotrigine, lacosamide
Focal — impaired awarenessAltered consciousness, automatisms (lip-smacking, picking)Carbamazepine, oxcarbazepine
Generalised tonic-clonicBilateral, sudden loss of consciousness, tonic then clonic phase, post-ictal confusionValproate, lamotrigine, levetiracetam
AbsenceBrief staring spells 5–30 sec, no post-ictal, 3Hz spike-wave on EEGEthosuximide, valproate, lamotrigine
Naturopathic InterventionEvidenceNotes
Ketogenic diet ★★★★Grade A for drug-resistant epilepsy — reduces seizures 50%+ in 50% of patientsStrictly supervised by neurologist + dietitian. Classic 4:1 fat:protein+carb ratio. Most evidence in children but used in adults too. Mechanism: ketone bodies alter GABA/glutamate balance
Magnesium★★★ GoodMg 400mg/day — NMDA receptor modulator. Deficiency lowers seizure threshold. Safe with most AEDs
Vit D3★★★ Good4000 IU/day — deficiency worsens seizure frequency. AEDs (phenytoin, carbamazepine, phenobarbital) induce Vit D metabolism → deficiency → osteomalacia
Omega-3★★★ Good3g EPA+DHA/day — membrane stabilising, anti-neuroinflammatory. Small RCTs show reduced seizure frequency
🟡 Herbs That Lower Seizure Threshold — AVOID
Avoid in epilepsy: Evening primrose oil (lowers seizure threshold — documented case reports), Borage oil (GLA lowers threshold), Ginkgo biloba (contains ginkgotoxin — pro-convulsant), Pennyroyal, Wormwood, Lobelia, high-dose camphor. Use with extreme caution: Kava (GABA-ergic — may interact with AEDs), St John's Wort (induces CYP3A4 — reduces levels of many AEDs including carbamazepine, lamotrigine, phenytoin by 25–40% → breakthrough seizures). Always disclose every supplement to the neurologist.

cards.title: Vertigo & Dizziness — Assessment & Management
cards._legacyHtml:
Dizziness — Rule Out Central Causes First ★
HINTS exam (Head Impulse, Nystagmus, Test of Skew) distinguishes peripheral from central vertigo. Central vertigo (stroke, tumour) requires emergency management. Peripheral vertigo (BPPV, labyrinthitis, Meniere's) is more common and amenable to naturopathic management.
TypeCharacterDurationTriggersNausea
BPPV ★ (most common)Brief intense rotatory vertigoSeconds (<1 min)Head position change (lying down, rolling over, looking up)Mild-moderate
Vestibular neuritisConstant vertigo, acute onsetDays–weeks, gradual improvementFollows URTI, spontaneousSevere
Meniere's diseaseEpisodic vertigo + tinnitus + unilateral hearing loss + ear fullness20 min–24 hrsStress, diet (salt, caffeine)Severe
Central (stroke/tumour) ★Continuous, severe, cannot walk, direction-changing nystagmusHours–permanentNo positional triggerVariable
Orthostatic hypotensionLightheadedness (not rotatory) on standingSeconds on standingRising from sitting/lyingAbsent
E
Epley Manoeuvre (BPPV) ★ 80% success rate in one session. Steps: (1) Sit upright, turn head 45° to affected side. (2) Lie back rapidly — head hanging 30° below bed, hold 30 sec. (3) Turn head 90° to opposite side, hold 30 sec. (4) Roll body to face-down position, hold 30 sec. (5) Sit up slowly. Prescribe 3 repetitions per session. Avoid lying flat for 48hrs after. Teach patient to self-perform at home.
M
Meniere's Disease — Naturopathic Protocol Low-sodium diet (<1500mg/day) — reduces endolymphatic pressure. Avoid: caffeine, alcohol, chocolate (vasoconstrictors). Stress management (MBSR — strong evidence for Meniere's). Ginkgo biloba 120mg BD — improves cochlear blood flow. Betahistine (prescriptive) — most effective medical treatment. Vit D deficiency common — supplement if deficient.
V
Vestibular Rehabilitation Gaze stabilisation exercises + habituation exercises for vestibular neuritis and chronic dizziness. Cawthorne-Cooksey exercises: eye movements → head movements → body movements — progressively challenge the vestibular system. Most effective when started early. Supervised vestibular physiotherapy is the gold standard for chronic vestibular hypofunction.
🟡 HINTS Exam for Acute Vertigo
HINTS (Head Impulse, Nystagmus, Test of Skew) — more sensitive than MRI in first 24hrs for detecting posterior fossa stroke. Benign (peripheral): abnormal head impulse test (catch-up saccade) + unidirectional horizontal nystagmus + no skew deviation. Dangerous (central): normal head impulse + direction-changing nystagmus + vertical skew deviation = stroke until proven otherwise → urgent MRI and neurology. Remember: "HINTS to central" = Head impulse Normal + Instability bidirectional Nystagmus + Test of Skew positive.

cards.title: Alzheimer's Prevention — ReCODE Protocol
cards._legacyHtml:
ReCODE — 36 Metabolic, Toxic & Inflammatory Factors
Dale Bredesen's ReCODE protocol treats Alzheimer's as a multifactorial network failure — aligned with naturopathic philosophy. Multiple published cases of objective cognitive improvement.
ReCODE DriverKey TestsIntervention
Inflammation (Type 1)hs-CRP, IL-6, fasting insulin, homocysteineAnti-inflammatory diet, omega-3 3g, curcumin, treat IR
Atrophic (Type 2 — hormonal)DHEAS, testosterone, E2, Vit D, B12, TSHOptimise all hormones, B12 methylcobalamin, Vit D 5000 IU
Toxic (Type 3)Heavy metals, mycotoxins, C4a, TGF-β1Detox protocol, sauna, glutathione, NAC, remove amalgam/mould
GlycotoxicHbA1c, fasting insulin, HOMA-IRLow-GI diet, berberine, TRE — "Type 3 diabetes" connection
1
Key NootropicsLion's Mane 500–3000mg/day ★★★ (NGF stimulation — RCT: MCI improvement, reversed on stopping). Bacopa 300–600mg ★★★ (memory consolidation — 8–12 weeks for effect). Phosphatidylserine 300mg ★★★ (FDA qualified health claim). Acetyl-L-carnitine 2g ★★. Omega-3 DHA 2g (hippocampal membrane).
2
Lifestyle ProtocolExercise ★★★ (#1 neurogenesis stimulus — BDNF). Sleep 7–9hrs ★★★ (glymphatic amyloid clearance requires NREM deep sleep). TRE 16:8 ★★★ (autophagy clears misfolded proteins). Cognitive engagement: language, music, chess. Social connection (isolation = dementia risk equivalent to 15 cigarettes/day). Morning Yoga Nidra + meditation daily (cortisol → hippocampal atrophy).

cards.title: Stroke — Prevention & Rehabilitation
cards._legacyHtml:
Stroke — Time Is Brain: 1.9 million neurons die per minute
FAST recognition and 108 call is the most critical action. Naturopathic role: primary/secondary prevention and post-acute rehabilitation (after 3–6 months stable). Never delay emergency stroke care.
TypeAcute TreatmentNaturopathic Prevention
Ischaemic (85%)tPA within 4.5hrs. Thrombectomy for LVO within 24hrs.Omega-3, Vit K2, Mg, anti-inflammatory, homocysteine management, BP <130/80
Haemorrhagic (15%)BP management, neurosurgery assessmentBP control ★★★ (most important). Avoid NSAIDs + fish oil >3g if history.
TIA — MAJOR warningSame-day emergency assessment. Aspirin 300mg immediately. ABCD2 ≥4 = admit.Treat identically to secondary stroke prevention — 10% stroke risk within 48hrs.
1
Secondary PreventionOmega-3 3g, methylfolate + B12 + B6 (homocysteine ↓), Vit E mixed tocopherols 400IU, Ginkgo 240mg (caution with anticoagulants). Conventional antiplatelet therapy (aspirin/clopidogrel) takes priority — naturopathic supplements adjunct only.
2
RehabilitationAcupuncture ★★★ (Cochrane: significant improvement in disability + motor function post-stroke). Yoga-based rehab ★★. Mediterranean diet reduces recurrence 30%. Screen for depression (30–40% post-stroke) — EPA omega-3 + exercise + bright light therapy improves rehab outcomes.

cards.title: Multiple Sclerosis — Naturopathic Adjunct
cards._legacyHtml:
MS — Adjunct to DMTs Only
Disease-modifying therapies reduce relapse rates but do not restore lost function. Naturopathic interventions complement DMTs by reducing inflammation, supporting mitochondrial function, and improving fatigue and QoL. Never advise stopping DMTs.
InterventionEvidenceProtocol
Exercise★★★ Cochrane Grade A — reduces fatigue, improves mobility, cognition, depressionAquatic exercise preferred (avoids Uhthoff's phenomenon). Resistance + aerobic 150 min/week. Start very gradually.
Vitamin D 5000–10,000 IU★★★ Low Vit D = ↑ MS risk. Coimbra protocol (40,000–100,000 IU) — specialist supervision only.Conservative: 5000–10,000 IU, monitor 25(OH)D + calcium quarterly.
Wahls Protocol★★ Open-label trial: significant fatigue + QoL improvement9 cups vegetables/day: 3 leafy greens, 3 coloured, 3 sulphur-rich. Eliminate gluten, dairy, eggs initially.
Alpha-lipoic acid★★ Phase 2 RCT: 1200mg/day ALA slowed brain atrophy 68% vs placebo over 2 yearsALA 600mg BD with food. Long-term — benefits cumulative.
Low dose naltrexone (LDN)★★ Multiple case series + small RCTs — QoL + fatigue improvementLDN 3–4.5mg at bedtime. TLR4 antagonism → reduces neuroinflammation.

cards.title: Headache Classification & DDx
cards._legacyHtml:
Red Flags — SNOOP Mnemonic
Systemic symptoms, Neurological deficit, Onset sudden ("thunderclap"), Older age >50 (new headache), Pattern change. Any red flag = urgent neurological assessment before naturopathic management.
TypeKey FeaturesNaturopathic Approach
Tension-type (most common)Bilateral, pressing, mild-moderate. No nausea. Band-like pressure.Mg 400mg ★★★, massage (trapezius trigger points), yoga, acupuncture ★★★
Migraine (2nd most common)Unilateral (60%), pulsating, moderate-severe, nausea, photophobia. Aura in 30%.Mg 400–600mg ★★★, riboflavin 400mg ★★★, CoQ10 300mg ★★, butterbur PE ★★★, acupuncture ★★★
Cluster headacheStrictly unilateral, periorbital, excruciating 15–180min. Autonomic features (ptosis, lacrimation). Restlessness.Melatonin 10mg bedtime ★★★, Vit D + calcium ★★. 100% O2 via mask (first-line abortive — refer for prescription). Avoid alcohol.
CervicogenicUnilateral, from neck movement. Tenderness C2/C3. Reduced cervical ROM.Manual therapy ★★★, acupuncture ★★★, postural correction, trigger point therapy
MOH (medication overuse)Chronic daily headache + analgesics/triptans >10–15 days/monthWITHDRAW offending medication (primary treatment). Bridge with IV Mg, B2, naproxen taper. Acupuncture during withdrawal.
Thunderclap headacheMaximum intensity within 60 seconds. "Worst headache of life."EMERGENCY — subarachnoid haemorrhage until proven otherwise. CT head immediately. Call 108.

cards.title: Autonomic Nervous System Disorders
cards._legacyHtml:
ANS Disorders — Underdiagnosed in Naturopathic Practice
POTS, dysautonomia, chronic fatigue, syncope, and Long COVID autonomic symptoms are frequently labelled "functional" or "psychosomatic." HRV assessment identifies these — they respond well to naturopathic management.
ConditionDefinitionNaturopathic Protocol
POTSHR ↑ ≥30 bpm within 10 min of standing (≥40 if <19). Common in young women, post-viral.Salt 6–10g/day + fluid 2–3L (volume expansion). Compression to waist. Graduated exercise (recumbent initially). HOB raised 30°. Melatonin 3mg (normalises circadian autonomic control).
Vasovagal SyncopeReflex bradycardia + vasodilation → cerebral hypoperfusion. Triggers: prolonged standing, emotion, pain, heat.Tilt training (30 min/day against wall — desensitises reflex). Salt + fluid. Counterpressure manoeuvres (crossed legs, tensed muscles) abort impending syncope.
Postural HypotensionSBP ↓ ≥20 or DBP ↓ ≥10 within 3 min of standingReview medications. HOB 10–20°. Salt + fluid. Small frequent meals. Licorice root (cortisol-sparing, raises BP). Rise slowly.
Diabetic Autonomic NeuropathyResting tachycardia, gastroparesis, bladder retention, sweating abnormalities, ED in menTight glucose ★★★. ALA 600mg, B12 methylcobalamin, Vit D, CoQ10. Gastroparesis: ginger 250mg QID ★★★, Iberogast ★★★.
🟡 HRV as Autonomic Assessment
Measurable with smartphone (HRV4Training, Elite HRV). Low HRV correlates with: chronic stress, CV risk, metabolic syndrome, cognitive decline. Most powerful HRV-improving interventions: Nadi Shodhana 20 min/day ★★★, slow breathing at 6 breaths/min, omega-3 3g/day, Vit D correction, sleep optimisation. Track weekly as objective treatment response biomarker.

cards.title: Vertigo & Dizziness — Assessment & Management
cards._legacyHtml:
Dizziness — Rule Out Central Causes First ★
HINTS exam (Head Impulse, Nystagmus, Test of Skew) distinguishes peripheral from central vertigo. Central vertigo (stroke, tumour) requires emergency management. Peripheral vertigo (BPPV, labyrinthitis, Meniere's) is more common and amenable to naturopathic management.
TypeCharacterDurationTriggersNausea
BPPV ★ (most common)Brief intense rotatory vertigoSeconds (<1 min)Head position change (lying down, rolling over, looking up)Mild-moderate
Vestibular neuritisConstant vertigo, acute onsetDays–weeks, gradual improvementFollows URTI, spontaneousSevere
Meniere's diseaseEpisodic vertigo + tinnitus + unilateral hearing loss + ear fullness20 min–24 hrsStress, diet (salt, caffeine)Severe
Central (stroke/tumour) ★Continuous, severe, cannot walk, direction-changing nystagmusHours–permanentNo positional triggerVariable
Orthostatic hypotensionLightheadedness (not rotatory) on standingSeconds on standingRising from sitting/lyingAbsent
E
Epley Manoeuvre (BPPV) ★ 80% success rate in one session. Steps: (1) Sit upright, turn head 45° to affected side. (2) Lie back rapidly — head hanging 30° below bed, hold 30 sec. (3) Turn head 90° to opposite side, hold 30 sec. (4) Roll body to face-down position, hold 30 sec. (5) Sit up slowly. Prescribe 3 repetitions per session. Avoid lying flat for 48hrs after. Teach patient to self-perform at home.
M
Meniere's Disease — Naturopathic Protocol Low-sodium diet (<1500mg/day) — reduces endolymphatic pressure. Avoid: caffeine, alcohol, chocolate (vasoconstrictors). Stress management (MBSR — strong evidence for Meniere's). Ginkgo biloba 120mg BD — improves cochlear blood flow. Betahistine (prescriptive) — most effective medical treatment. Vit D deficiency common — supplement if deficient.
V
Vestibular Rehabilitation Gaze stabilisation exercises + habituation exercises for vestibular neuritis and chronic dizziness. Cawthorne-Cooksey exercises: eye movements → head movements → body movements — progressively challenge the vestibular system. Most effective when started early. Supervised vestibular physiotherapy is the gold standard for chronic vestibular hypofunction.
🟡 HINTS Exam for Acute Vertigo
HINTS (Head Impulse, Nystagmus, Test of Skew) — more sensitive than MRI in first 24hrs for detecting posterior fossa stroke. Benign (peripheral): abnormal head impulse test (catch-up saccade) + unidirectional horizontal nystagmus + no skew deviation. Dangerous (central): normal head impulse + direction-changing nystagmus + vertical skew deviation = stroke until proven otherwise → urgent MRI and neurology. Remember: "HINTS to central" = Head impulse Normal + Instability bidirectional Nystagmus + Test of Skew positive.

cards.title: Autonomic Nervous System Disorders
cards._legacyHtml:
ANS Disorders — Underdiagnosed in Naturopathic Practice
POTS, dysautonomia, chronic fatigue, syncope, and Long COVID autonomic symptoms are frequently labelled "functional" or "psychosomatic." HRV assessment identifies these — they respond well to naturopathic management.
ConditionDefinitionNaturopathic Protocol
POTSHR ↑ ≥30 bpm within 10 min of standing (≥40 if <19). Common in young women, post-viral.Salt 6–10g/day + fluid 2–3L (volume expansion). Compression to waist. Graduated exercise (recumbent initially). HOB raised 30°. Melatonin 3mg (normalises circadian autonomic control).
Vasovagal SyncopeReflex bradycardia + vasodilation → cerebral hypoperfusion. Triggers: prolonged standing, emotion, pain, heat.Tilt training (30 min/day against wall — desensitises reflex). Salt + fluid. Counterpressure manoeuvres (crossed legs, tensed muscles) abort impending syncope.
Postural HypotensionSBP ↓ ≥20 or DBP ↓ ≥10 within 3 min of standingReview medications. HOB 10–20°. Salt + fluid. Small frequent meals. Licorice root (cortisol-sparing, raises BP). Rise slowly.
Diabetic Autonomic NeuropathyResting tachycardia, gastroparesis, bladder retention, sweating abnormalities, ED in menTight glucose ★★★. ALA 600mg, B12 methylcobalamin, Vit D, CoQ10. Gastroparesis: ginger 250mg QID ★★★, Iberogast ★★★.
🟡 HRV as Autonomic Assessment
Measurable with smartphone (HRV4Training, Elite HRV). Low HRV correlates with: chronic stress, CV risk, metabolic syndrome, cognitive decline. Most powerful HRV-improving interventions: Nadi Shodhana 20 min/day ★★★, slow breathing at 6 breaths/min, omega-3 3g/day, Vit D correction, sleep optimisation. Track weekly as objective treatment response biomarker.

cards.title: Memory & Cognitive Health — Naturopathic Prevention
cards._legacyHtml:
Dementia is 40% Preventable
Lancet Commission 2020: 12 modifiable risk factors account for 40% of dementia cases worldwide. Naturopathic medicine addresses most of these.
InterventionDoseMechanismEvidence
Aerobic exercise150 min/week + 2x resistanceBDNF up, hippocampal neurogenesis, cerebrovascular upStrongest single intervention
MIND dietLifelong patternAntioxidants, anti-inflammatory, gut-brain axis53% reduced Alzheimer risk
Omega-3 DHA2g DHA/day (algae preferred)Neuronal membrane fluidity, anti-neuroinflammationGood for prevention
Lion's Mane mushroom500–1000mg extract TDSNGF (nerve growth factor) synthesis stimulationGood — mild cognitive impairment RCT
Bacopa monnieri300–450mg standardised/daySynaptic transmission up, acetylcholinesterase inhibitionGood — improves memory acquisition
Sleep 7–8 hoursConsistent scheduleGlymphatic clearance of amyloid-b and tau during deep sleepCritical — poor sleep raises Alzheimer risk 25%
Social engagementDaily meaningful interactionCognitive reserve, neuroplasticityIsolation raises dementia risk 60%
🟡 The Glymphatic System
Discovered in 2012, the glymphatic system clears brain waste including amyloid-b and tau proteins during deep sleep (NREM3) — 10x more active than waking. CSF flows through periarterial spaces, exchanges with interstitial fluid. Chronic sleep deprivation leads to amyloid accumulation and Alzheimer risk. Sleep on your SIDE (lateral position increases glymphatic flow 25% vs supine). This is why sleep is the most important dementia prevention intervention available.

cards.title: Alzheimer's Prevention — ReCODE Protocol
cards._legacyHtml:
ReCODE — 36 Metabolic, Toxic & Inflammatory Factors
Dale Bredesen's ReCODE protocol treats Alzheimer's as a multifactorial network failure — aligned with naturopathic philosophy. Multiple published cases of objective cognitive improvement.
ReCODE DriverKey TestsIntervention
Inflammation (Type 1)hs-CRP, IL-6, fasting insulin, homocysteineAnti-inflammatory diet, omega-3 3g, curcumin, treat IR
Atrophic (Type 2 — hormonal)DHEAS, testosterone, E2, Vit D, B12, TSHOptimise all hormones, B12 methylcobalamin, Vit D 5000 IU
Toxic (Type 3)Heavy metals, mycotoxins, C4a, TGF-β1Detox protocol, sauna, glutathione, NAC, remove amalgam/mould
GlycotoxicHbA1c, fasting insulin, HOMA-IRLow-GI diet, berberine, TRE — "Type 3 diabetes" connection
1
Key NootropicsLion's Mane 500–3000mg/day ★★★ (NGF stimulation — RCT: MCI improvement, reversed on stopping). Bacopa 300–600mg ★★★ (memory consolidation — 8–12 weeks for effect). Phosphatidylserine 300mg ★★★ (FDA qualified health claim). Acetyl-L-carnitine 2g ★★. Omega-3 DHA 2g (hippocampal membrane).
2
Lifestyle ProtocolExercise ★★★ (#1 neurogenesis stimulus — BDNF). Sleep 7–9hrs ★★★ (glymphatic amyloid clearance requires NREM deep sleep). TRE 16:8 ★★★ (autophagy clears misfolded proteins). Cognitive engagement: language, music, chess. Social connection (isolation = dementia risk equivalent to 15 cigarettes/day). Morning Yoga Nidra + meditation daily (cortisol → hippocampal atrophy).

cards.title: Multiple Sclerosis — Naturopathic Adjunct
cards._legacyHtml:
MS — Adjunct to DMTs Only
Disease-modifying therapies reduce relapse rates but do not restore lost function. Naturopathic interventions complement DMTs by reducing inflammation, supporting mitochondrial function, and improving fatigue and QoL. Never advise stopping DMTs.
InterventionEvidenceProtocol
Exercise★★★ Cochrane Grade A — reduces fatigue, improves mobility, cognition, depressionAquatic exercise preferred (avoids Uhthoff's phenomenon). Resistance + aerobic 150 min/week. Start very gradually.
Vitamin D 5000–10,000 IU★★★ Low Vit D = ↑ MS risk. Coimbra protocol (40,000–100,000 IU) — specialist supervision only.Conservative: 5000–10,000 IU, monitor 25(OH)D + calcium quarterly.
Wahls Protocol★★ Open-label trial: significant fatigue + QoL improvement9 cups vegetables/day: 3 leafy greens, 3 coloured, 3 sulphur-rich. Eliminate gluten, dairy, eggs initially.
Alpha-lipoic acid★★ Phase 2 RCT: 1200mg/day ALA slowed brain atrophy 68% vs placebo over 2 yearsALA 600mg BD with food. Long-term — benefits cumulative.
Low dose naltrexone (LDN)★★ Multiple case series + small RCTs — QoL + fatigue improvementLDN 3–4.5mg at bedtime. TLR4 antagonism → reduces neuroinflammation.

cards.title: Parkinson's Disease — Naturopathic Adjunct Care
cards._legacyHtml:
Parkinson's Disease — Medical Management Essential ★
PD is a progressive neurodegenerative disorder requiring neurologist supervision. Levodopa/carbidopa and other dopaminergic agents are essential — naturopathic interventions are adjuncts to slow progression, manage symptoms, and improve quality of life.
FeatureDetails
Cardinal motor features (TRAP)Tremor (resting, 4–6Hz, pill-rolling), Rigidity (cogwheel), Akinesia/Bradykinesia, Postural instability (late)
Non-motor features ★Constipation (begins 10–20 yrs before motor symptoms), anosmia, REM sleep behaviour disorder, depression, cognitive decline, autonomic dysfunction
DiagnosisClinical — UK Brain Bank criteria. DaTscan (dopamine transporter SPECT) for confirmation. No blood test or biomarker.
InterventionEvidenceProtocol
Exercise ★★★★Strongest neuroprotective intervention. BDNF ↑, neuroplasticity, motor function preservation150 min/week aerobic + twice-weekly resistance. Tai chi (balance +++), boxing (HIIT for PD — Rock Steady Boxing), dance therapy. Start early — more effective in early-stage PD
CoQ10★★★ Good1200mg/day — mitochondrial support. PD = mitochondrial dysfunction in substantia nigra. Early trials showed slowed progression (not replicated in large RCT)
Green tea (EGCG)★★★ Good — observational + lab3–5 cups/day or EGCG 300–400mg/day. Neuroprotective — MAO-B inhibition, iron chelation, antioxidant
Mucuna pruriens ★★★★ GoodNatural levodopa source — 15% L-DOPA content. 15–30g seed powder. Discuss with neurologist — interacts with levodopa medications. Faster onset, possibly smoother than synthetic levodopa.
Mediterranean diet★★★ GoodReduces PD risk 25–30% in observational studies. Anti-neuroinflammatory, antioxidant
Constipation management★★★★ CriticalPrebiotics/probiotics — gut-brain axis. Psyllium + magnesium + hydration. Constipation = major non-motor burden and affects levodopa absorption
🟡 Protein & Levodopa Interaction
High-protein meals significantly reduce levodopa absorption — amino acids compete for the same intestinal transporter (LAT1). Practical advice for patients on levodopa: take medication 30–60 min BEFORE meals. Distribute protein intake to evening meal (when motor control is less critical). Avoid large protein-rich meals around "on" periods. This simple dietary modification can significantly improve motor fluctuations. Also: Vit B6 at doses >50mg can reduce levodopa efficacy — use P-5-P (pyridoxal-5-phosphate) form if supplementing.

cards.title: Epilepsy & Seizure Management — Naturopathic Reference
cards._legacyHtml:
CRITICAL — Never Reduce Anti-Epileptic Drugs (AEDs) Without Neurologist
Abrupt AED withdrawal risks status epilepticus — life-threatening. Naturopathic interventions are adjuncts. Always disclose ALL supplements to the patient's neurologist — many herbs lower seizure threshold or interact with AEDs.
Seizure ClassificationFeaturesCommon AEDs
Focal (partial) — awareLocalised — sensory, motor, autonomic, psychic. Consciousness preservedCarbamazepine, lamotrigine, lacosamide
Focal — impaired awarenessAltered consciousness, automatisms (lip-smacking, picking)Carbamazepine, oxcarbazepine
Generalised tonic-clonicBilateral, sudden loss of consciousness, tonic then clonic phase, post-ictal confusionValproate, lamotrigine, levetiracetam
AbsenceBrief staring spells 5–30 sec, no post-ictal, 3Hz spike-wave on EEGEthosuximide, valproate, lamotrigine
Naturopathic InterventionEvidenceNotes
Ketogenic diet ★★★★Grade A for drug-resistant epilepsy — reduces seizures 50%+ in 50% of patientsStrictly supervised by neurologist + dietitian. Classic 4:1 fat:protein+carb ratio. Most evidence in children but used in adults too. Mechanism: ketone bodies alter GABA/glutamate balance
Magnesium★★★ GoodMg 400mg/day — NMDA receptor modulator. Deficiency lowers seizure threshold. Safe with most AEDs
Vit D3★★★ Good4000 IU/day — deficiency worsens seizure frequency. AEDs (phenytoin, carbamazepine, phenobarbital) induce Vit D metabolism → deficiency → osteomalacia
Omega-3★★★ Good3g EPA+DHA/day — membrane stabilising, anti-neuroinflammatory. Small RCTs show reduced seizure frequency
🟡 Herbs That Lower Seizure Threshold — AVOID
Avoid in epilepsy: Evening primrose oil (lowers seizure threshold — documented case reports), Borage oil (GLA lowers threshold), Ginkgo biloba (contains ginkgotoxin — pro-convulsant), Pennyroyal, Wormwood, Lobelia, high-dose camphor. Use with extreme caution: Kava (GABA-ergic — may interact with AEDs), St John's Wort (induces CYP3A4 — reduces levels of many AEDs including carbamazepine, lamotrigine, phenytoin by 25–40% → breakthrough seizures). Always disclose every supplement to the neurologist.

cards.title: Stroke — Prevention & Rehabilitation
cards._legacyHtml:
Stroke — Time Is Brain: 1.9 million neurons die per minute
FAST recognition and 108 call is the most critical action. Naturopathic role: primary/secondary prevention and post-acute rehabilitation (after 3–6 months stable). Never delay emergency stroke care.
TypeAcute TreatmentNaturopathic Prevention
Ischaemic (85%)tPA within 4.5hrs. Thrombectomy for LVO within 24hrs.Omega-3, Vit K2, Mg, anti-inflammatory, homocysteine management, BP <130/80
Haemorrhagic (15%)BP management, neurosurgery assessmentBP control ★★★ (most important). Avoid NSAIDs + fish oil >3g if history.
TIA — MAJOR warningSame-day emergency assessment. Aspirin 300mg immediately. ABCD2 ≥4 = admit.Treat identically to secondary stroke prevention — 10% stroke risk within 48hrs.
1
Secondary PreventionOmega-3 3g, methylfolate + B12 + B6 (homocysteine ↓), Vit E mixed tocopherols 400IU, Ginkgo 240mg (caution with anticoagulants). Conventional antiplatelet therapy (aspirin/clopidogrel) takes priority — naturopathic supplements adjunct only.
2
RehabilitationAcupuncture ★★★ (Cochrane: significant improvement in disability + motor function post-stroke). Yoga-based rehab ★★. Mediterranean diet reduces recurrence 30%. Screen for depression (30–40% post-stroke) — EPA omega-3 + exercise + bright light therapy improves rehab outcomes.
